text: This paper provides an overview of the contemporary leadership in the Armed Forces of Bosnia and Herzegovina (AF BiH). Data from a survey of 200 soldiers were used for descriptive analysis of the degree of motivational leadership in the AF BiH, the description of the military environment, the description of the military leaders and the positive effects of leadership in the organization. The results of the descriptive analysis show a high degree of consensus of the soldiers in relation to all researched variables, and the results coincide with theoretical expectations. High degree of positive agreement on the professional character of military leaders and positive effects of military leadership have been achieved. High degree of consensus has also been achieved in relation to the motivation of soldiers in the military environment characterized by routine tasks and standard operational procedures, which is in line with the characteristics of military profession. The research results show that the forces in question use a complex model of leadership that covers task, team and organizational leadership, while the phenomenon of shared leadership is also visible.
